name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist specializing in mass spectrometry-based analytical characterization, with extensive experience in LC-MS method development, small molecule analysis, and quantitative bioanalytical methods. Proven track record of developing robust analytical methods for complex molecular systems.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading chemical process development and optimization for novel bio-based polymer systems, applying statistical experimental design to identify critical process parameters, improve yield and product quality, and establish reproducible process specifications for scale-up.

Assessed material-level reliability risks in functional electrochromic systems by identifying high-risk failure modes including oxidative degradation, interface delamination, and redox instability, applying systematic root cause analysis to develop design and process mitigations.

Developed and executed stress test conditions based on material physics of failure, evaluating long-term stability and degradation behavior of 15+ organic and organometallic material variants under controlled electrochemical and optical stress conditions.

Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications, developing surface preparation protocols and CVD-based deposition strategies to achieve controlled film morphology and device performance.

Developed and optimized CVD-based polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, establishing process control parameters and characterizing film growth kinetics to improve coating reproducibility and uniformity.

Managed process development laboratory operations for a 15-member research group; implemented equipment tracking and calibration systems improving instrument uptime by 30%, developed SOPs and safety protocols, and coordinated cross-functional resource planning across multiple concurrent projects.

projects.title: Electrochromic Material Reliability Study
projects.description: Developed systematic characterization framework using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, and cyclic voltammetry to evaluate 15+ organic thin-film material variants. Identified 3 distinct failure modes through physics-of-failure methodology and accelerated aging tests.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, Cyclic Voltammetry, Spectroelectrochemistry, Statistical DOE, Accelerated Testing

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Co-led process development for molecular thin-film coatings for defense optics applications. Developed surface preparation protocols and CVD deposition strategies to achieve controlled film morphology and device performance.
projects.tech: CVD Deposition, Surface Functionalization, Process Development

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Scale-Up Process
projects.description: Leading process development for bio-based polymer systems from research through scale-up. Applying statistical DOE to optimize critical parameters and establishing reproducible manufacturing specifications.
projects.tech: Polymer Chemistry, GPC/SEC, Statistical DOE, Process Specifications, Scale-Up

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.

skills.Reliability and Failure Analysis: Failure mode identification, root cause analysis, physics of failure, materials degradation characterization, stress test development, design and process mitigation, FMEA principles, accelerated testing methodology
skills.Materials Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR spectrophotometry, ellipsometry, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, TGA, DSC, NMR, GPC, QCM-D, GC-MS, LC-MS
skills.Statistical Analysis: DOE, Statistical Process Control (SPC, Coursera 2026), JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel (advanced), statistical data analysis, Weibull analysis (familiarity)
skills.Materials Expertise: Organic and organometallic thin-film materials, PVD-adjacent optical coatings, polymer systems, surface functionalization, structure-property-processing relationships
skills.Project Management: Multi-project management, cross-functional collaboration, technical documentation, stakeholder communication

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
